results.label: Version flag works (`--version` plus short alias)
Version flag works (`--version` plus short alias)
Non-interactive gate flag advertised in --help
results.evidence: target satisfies P1 via alternative gate (help-on-bare or stdin-primary)

results.label: Flags advertise env-var bindings in --help
results.evidence: 11 flag(s) found in --help but no `[env: NAME]` bindings advertised

results.label: Pager-using CLI ships --no-pager escape hatch
results.evidence: pager referenced in --help but no --no-pager escape hatch advertised

results.label: Secret-bearing flags expose stdin or *-file companion
Structured-output CLI exposes its schema at runtime
results.evidence: CLI emits structured output but exposes no `schema` subcommand or `--schema` flag at top level or nested one level deep. Agents need a runtime-discoverable schema to pin against shape changes.

results.label: --json / --jsonl short aliases for --output
results.evidence: no --json or --jsonl short alias found. Agents and pipelines benefit from short forms alongside the canonical `--output` enum.

results.label: Subcommand verbs follow community-standard names
results.evidence: no subcommands parsed from --help

results.label: Skill bundle has install path (`tool skill install [<host>]`)
`--raw` flag for pipe-safe unformatted output
results.evidence: no `--raw` flag advertised. MAY-tier — useful for pipelines that want to strip formatting before piping to other tools.

results.label: `examples` subcommand or `--examples` flag for curated usage patterns
results.evidence: no `examples` subcommand or `--examples` flag found. MAY-tier — a curated usage block keeps agents from hunting through long help text.

results.label: `--color` flag for explicit color control
results.evidence: no `--color` flag advertised. MAY-tier — `auto|always|never` lets agents and pipelines override the TTY-based default.

results.label: `--limit` / `--max-results` flag for list operations
results.evidence: no list-style subcommand detected (list/ls/search/query/find/show/get); vacuous skip for the list-only SHOULD.

results.label: Cursor-based pagination flags for list traversal
results.evidence: no list-style subcommand detected; vacuous skip for the list-only MAY.

results.label: `--help` advertises default values for flags
results.evidence: no default-value annotations found in --help. SHOULD-tier — agents reading help text need to see what value a flag falls back to when omitted (`[default: <value>]` per clap convention).

results.label: Short `-h` summary differs from `--help` long form
results.evidence: `-h` and `--help` produce byte-identical output. SHOULD-tier — clap renders the short summary on `-h` and the full description on `--help` when `long_about` is set; collapsing them gives agents no concise list-level grep target.

results.label: Input-accepting commands read from stdin when no file is given
results.evidence: no input-accepting subcommand detected (process/parse/convert/transform/analyze/validate/format/lint/audit); vacuous skip for the conditional SHOULD.

results.label: Subcommand naming follows a consistent verb/noun convention
results.evidence: fewer than 2 user-defined subcommands; vacuous skip for the conditional SHOULD.

results.label: `--timeout` flag for long-running operations
results.evidence: no long-running subcommand detected (serve/daemon/watch/tail/monitor/follow/run/start/stream); vacuous skip for the conditional SHOULD.

results.label: Bad invocation exits with structured usage-error code (2)
results.evidence: bad invocation exited with code 1. The 0/1/2/77/78 convention reserves code 2 for usage errors; using a different non-zero code (often 1) blurs the distinction between usage errors and general failure.

results.label: Error messages include a hint or remediation phrase
Errors emit JSON envelope with `error`/`kind`/`message` under `--output json`
results.evidence: binary does not advertise `--output json` in --help; MUST applies only to CLIs that opt into the JSON contract.

results.label: `--output json` produces JSON-formatted errors
results.evidence: binary does not advertise `--output json` in --help; SHOULD applies only to CLIs that opt into the JSON contract.

results.label: JSON success and error envelopes share their non-payload key set
results.evidence: binary does not advertise `--output json` in --help; envelope-consistency only applies to CLIs that opt into the JSON contract.

results.label: Each subcommand's `--help` ships at least one invocation example
results.evidence: binary has no subcommands; MUST applies conditionally to CLIs that use them.

results.label: Help text pairs human and `--output json` example invocations
results.evidence: no paired text + `--output json` example found within 5 lines in top-level or any subcommand `--help`. Pairing keeps agents from reverse-engineering the JSON invocation from the text one.

results.label: Operations are subcommands, not verb-shaped flags
Destructive subcommands require `--force` or `--yes`
results.evidence: no destructive subcommands detected; MUST applies conditionally to CLIs with destructive operations.

results.label: Read and write surfaces are both visible in subcommand list
results.evidence: no recognizable read or write subcommand verbs; the read/write distinction is unobservable from the help surface alone.

results.label: Help text advertises TTY-aware verbosity behavior
results.evidence: no TTY-aware language found in `--help`. MAY-tier — automatic verbosity reduction when stdout is piped or redirected lets agents skip the explicit `--quiet` flag. Behavioral probes cannot simulate a real TTY without a pty crate, so this audit relies on documented intent.
